ai-w3-t1.answer: AI code review scans for smells, naming, structure — fast first pass. You decide what to accept. It's a linter with opinions, not a senior engineer.
ai-w3-t1.realWorld: Paste Student.java; AI flags public fields — you refactor to private + getters.

ai-w3-t2.answer: Code smells: long methods, duplicate code, magic numbers, god classes, poor names. AI spots many; you prioritize fixes.
ai-w3-t2.realWorld: Three classes named Manager1, Manager2 — rename before feature work piles on.

ai-w3-t3.answer: Java naming: classes PascalCase, methods/variables camelCase, constants UPPER_SNAKE. Names reveal intent: calculateTotal not calc.
ai-w3-t3.realWorld: isActive beats flag1 — boolean names answer a yes/no question.

ai-w3-t4.answer: Readable code: short methods, clear names, consistent formatting. If you need a comment to explain what, rename instead.
ai-w3-t4.realWorld: AI suggests extracting a 40-line main() into processInput() and displayMenu().

ai-w3-t5.answer: Maintainable code: easy to change without breaking. Small classes, single responsibility, tests. Today's shortcut is tomorrow's bug.
ai-w3-t5.realWorld: Hardcoded tax rate in 5 places — one constant or config file saves future edits.

ai-w3-t6.answer: AI review is fast but misses business context and team conventions. Human review catches 'technically fine but wrong approach.'
ai-w3-t6.realWorld: AI approves clever one-liner; teammate says 'we don't use Streams in legacy module.'

ai-w3-t7.answer: Ask AI to improve class design: 'Review BankAccount for encapsulation and single responsibility.' Implement one suggestion at a time.
ai-w3-t7.realWorld: Split Account and Transaction classes — AI draws the line; you verify domain logic.

ai-w3-t8.answer: AI drafts JavaDoc and README from code. You fix inaccuracies. Good for boilerplate, not for architecture decisions.
ai-w3-t8.realWorld: Generate JavaDoc for public API methods; delete wrong @param names AI invented.

ai-w3-t9.answer: Refactor with AI: ask for extract method, rename, remove duplication. Run program after each small change.
ai-w3-t9.realWorld: Extract validateInput() from main — one refactor, one test run.

ai-w3-t10.answer: AI can suggest UML from class lists. Validate relationships match your code — arrows and cardinalities often wrong.
ai-w3-t10.realWorld: Student 1—* Course diagram — check if your code actually supports many courses.
